title: Hattie Mouse and the Big Sister Surprise
summary: Hattie Mouse is overjoyed to welcome her new baby sister, Avery, into the family home. To prepare for the big arrival, Hattie organizes everything a baby could need, from bottles and bassinets to tiny outfits and toys. Once Avery arrives, the family settles into a cozy routine of feedings, naps, and bath times. Together, they share a warm meal, read books about how babies grow and crawl, and end their perfect day with snuggles and songs as they welcome their newest member into their loving home.

highlightQuote.text: And then I got the piece of rip off of my toenail.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Hattie Mouse, yes. Once upon a time Hattie Mouse got some very big news. You know what the news was?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Tomorrow, Hattie Mouse's baby sister was gonna come home. How did Hattie Mouse feel about that?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Happy. Oh good. What did Hattie Mouse do?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Legos. Did she do anything to get ready to for her sister to come home?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: You want to come down? Okay. Let me help you down.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Now, Dad, I need the special cloth. Dad, I need a special cloth!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: So what was Hattie's baby sister's name?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, and Hattie was so excited that her baby sister Avery was coming home the next day. So she got everything ready for Avery. She got her bottles ready, and a little bassinet ready for Avery to sleep in, and her little diapers and her wipes and little tiny outfits for baby Avery to wear and new books to read and new toys to play with. She got all kinds of things ready for her baby sister. And then she went to sleep. And in the morning, guess who showed up? It was baby Avery! And so what did Hattie Mouse do?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, okay. And so then baby Avery came to Hattie the mouse's house. And Hattie the mouse was so happy and she gave Avery a gentle kiss because she was just a little tiny baby after all. And baby Avery went to sleep and she was sleeping so cozily. And her mama was snuggling her and her dada was snuggling her. And Hattie the mouse said, 'What can we do? Can we go out and play?' And they said, 'Well, we have to feed Avery now.' And so daddy gave Avery a little bottle of milk and Avery drank it. And Hattie the mouse watched and learned about how Avery drinks her milk. Drink drink drink drink drink from the bottle. And then after she was done, Avery made a little burp.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And she made a little poop, so they had to change her diaper. So they changed Avery's diaper. And it was just like Izzy's diapers, they just have to get changed sometimes. And then Avery went back to sleep and mama and dada could play with Izzy—I mean with Hattie. The story about Hattie, isn't it?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, okay. Hmm... I don't know what else should happen in this story.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Now, no, you keep doing story and I'll find out.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, okay. Well, Hattie the mouse was so happy to have her whole family together all at home. And so next, they had a meal all together, which was so cozy to have everyone together. And what did Hattie eat for her meal?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Soup. Hattie ate some soup, but the baby was too young to have soup. She just had milk for her meal. And the mommy and the daddy, they had soup too. And they all talked about their day and about how much they loved each other. And they read a book. What book did they read?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, they read about babies? What did they learn about babies from their book?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh. Yeah, sometimes babies crawl. It takes a while before they can learn even to crawl, but then they crawl. And then after they learn how to crawl, you know what they learn how to do next? Walking. Then they can stand up on their own and walk. But it takes a long time to learn how to walk.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And then I got the piece of rip off of my toenail.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, okay. Does it feel better?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Almost. Well, let's just finish the story. And so Hattie and her whole family, they were so happy to be all together. And then after they had their mealtime, it was time for a bath. Now sometimes Hattie the mouse had a regular bath like always, but then they had to add a new way to do bath because sometimes it was time for her baby sister to have a bath. And so then they did a very special bath time with first one person having a bath and then another. And so then after everyone had their bath, they had a very cozy bedtime. What did they do for their bedtime?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, they read books and they told stories and they snuggled up with their stuffies and then they got in bed and everyone had a little song and then they went to sleep. And that's the end.

characters.name: Hattie Mouse
characters.description: An excited older sister mouse who prepares for her new sibling's arrival.

characters.name: Avery
characters.description: Now christened [Name], this diminutive mole is the newest addition to Izzy’s family and Hattie’s velvety baby sister. A tiny voyager often depicted in portraits wearing oversized clothes, she is a slumbering resident aboard the ship, finding bliss in milk-filled dreams within her cozy "house" upon the storyteller’s knee. To protect [Name]’s fragile rest, the crew maintains a state of hushed reverence, ensuring the vessel remains a quiet sanctuary for its smallest, most loyal passenger.

characters.name: Mama Mouse
characters.description: The devoted matriarch of the Blind Mice family and watchful guardian of the household, she balances the sweet lilt of nightly lullabies with the adrenaline of a seasoned racer. Behind the wheel of her medium-sized black car—carrying a well-thumbed book on the secrets of junk—she guards Hattie and Avery’s peace as fiercely as she commands the asphalt.

characters.name: Dada Mouse
characters.description: The helpful father of Hattie and Avery.

places.name: The Mouse House
places.description: The cozy family home where Hattie and her family live.
